City of National City
Otfice of the City Attorney
1243 National City Boulevard, National City, CA 920504397
George H. Eiser, lll-City Attorney 
(619) 336-4220
July 13, 1990
scott Hallabrin, Esq
Acting ceneral Counsel
Fair Political Practices Comnission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacranento, CA 95804
Re: Request for Commission Opinion
Dear !1r. Hal labr in :
On behalf of National City Mayor ceorge Waters, Vice-Mayor Jessie
E. Van Deventer, Councilman Michael R. DaL1a, and Councifman Fred
Pruittr a Commission opinion is hereby requested pursuant to
covernment Code Section 83114(a) and Sections L8320-18327 of the
ReguLations of the Fair Political Practices Commission. I have
been authorized to make this request by action of the City Council
taken on June 26, 1990.
EACTUAL BACKGROUND
The City of National City is located in southern San Diego County.
The City has a population of approximately 55,000r and is 8.55
sguare miles in area. The City's land area is approximately 7.34
square miles, or 41697 acres, in area. The City is bounded by the
City of San Diego on the North, the City of Chula Vista on the
South, the Pacific Ocean on the West, and the County of San Diego
on the East.
The City of National City is a general IaI., city with a directly-
elected mayor and four councilmenbers. The current City Council
is comprised of Mayor George H. Waters, Vice-Mayor Jessie E. Van
Deventer, Councilman Michael R. DaIIa, Councilman Ralph Inzunza,
anil Councilman Fred Pruitt.
Pursuant to the Community Redevelopment taw, Health and Safety Code
Section 33000 et. seg., there is in each community a public body
known as the redevelopment agency of the community (liealth and
Safety Code Section 33100). The redevelopment agency in each
cornmunity is nactivatedn by enactment of an ordinance by the
Legislative body declaring that there is a need for an agency to
function in the community (Section 33101). The Redevelopment


Agency of the City of National City was activated on April IL,
1967, pursuant to Ordinance No. 1167. On October 14, 1975, the
Community Development Commission of the City of National City, a
combined redevelopment agency and housing authority, was formed
pursuant to Ordj.nance No. 1484. AIso pursuant to Ordinance No.
1484, the City Council of the City of National City declared itself
to be the Community Development Commission of the City of National
City.
Beginning in 1969, a series of redevelopment projects !,rere adopted
in the communityr namely, the E. J. Christman Business & fndustrial
Park I Redevelopment Project; the E. J. Christman Business &
Industrial Park Redevelopment Project, Amendment No. Ifi the South
Bay Town & Country Redevelopment Projecti and the Center City
RedeveLopment Project. These projects were merged with additional
land to form the National City Downtown Redevelopmment project,
pursuant to Ordinance No. 1752, adopted on December I, 1981. Minor
amendments to the Redevelopment P1an, which added a total of
approximately three acres to the Project r w€r€ approved by
Ordinance No. 1821r adopted on l,lay 22, 1984, (Amendment I), and by
Ordinance No. ]851, adopted on April 16, 1985 (Amendnent II). 
with
the 1984 and 1985 arnendments, the National City 
Downtown
Redevelopment Project consists of approximately 2 r083 acres
approximately 448 of the Cityrs land area.
A copy of Lhe National City Downtown Redevelopment Project
Redevelopment P1an, (which includes as Exhibit "1n a Project Area
Boundary Map) , is enclosed as Exhibit 
"A. "
The documents
pertaining to Amendments f and II of the Redevelopment Plan are
enclosed as Exhibit "B.rr
A significant 
feature of the Redevelopment PLan is that the
Community Development Commission has been granted the power of
eminent domain only in those areas which are designated as
nacquisition areas." (See Section 2(b), "Redevelopment Land, this
Project,n and Exhibits 4 and 5 to 1981 Redevelopment Plan.)
Currently, 
Amendmen t III 
to Lhe Redevelopment Plan is being
prepared. A workshop meeti.ng for the entire Community Development
Commission board is scheduled for JuIy 18, 1990. The Executive
Director of the Community Development Commission has prepared a
written report setting forth the parameters of the proposed
Amendment ffI, 
including a map showing proposed Acquisition Areas
"A" through "G. " The distinct elements of the proposed amendment
to the Redevelopment Plan may be considered, and approved or
rejected, separately. A copy of the Executive Directorrs report
is enclosed as Exhibit 'rC."
It should be noted thal Acquisition Area nBn of the proposed
Amendment III is located outside the boundaries of the existing
Project Area. Acquisition Areas "A",,Cn, nDn, nEn, np,r and 'rGr
are located within the boundaries of the existing project Area.


REAL PROPERTY INTERESTS OF NATIONAL CITY COUNCILMEMBERS
Mayor George H. waters o$rns the following interests in real
property within the city 
of National Cityr and within the
boundaries of the Project Area of the National City Downtown
Redevelopment Project Redevelopment Plan: 1) A fee interest in
real property locateil at 1436 Highland Avenue. The fair market
value of the property is greater than $100,000. The property is
improved with a commercial building, which is rented, producing an
annual incorne of between 91000 and $10,000. 2) A fee interest in
real property located at 1440 Highland Avenue. The fair market
value of the property is greater than $100,000. The Property is
improved wj.th a residence, r,rhich is rented, producing annual income
of betr^/een $1000 and $10,000. 3) A fee interest in real Property
located aL 1442 Highland Avenue. The fair market value of the
property is greater than $100.000. The property is improved with
a commercial building, which is rented, producing annual income of
bet$reen $1000 and $10r000. 4) A fee interest in real Property
located at 1444-1445 Highland Avenue. The fair market value of the
property is greater than $100,000. The property is iinproved with
a commercial building, which is used as the place of business for
a comnercial office tnachines sales and service business of which
Mayor waters is the proprietor.
In addition to the above real property interests, Mayor waters owns
a personal residence aE L342 Carol P1ace, which is within the City
limits but outside the boundaries of the Redevelopment Project
Area.
None of Mayor Waters' properties is located within 2500 feet of any
of the acquisition areas designated in Amendment III of the
Redevelopment PLan.
Vice-Mayor Jessie E. Van Deventer o$rns the following i.nterests in
real property within the City of National City, and within the
boundaries of the Project Area of the National City Downtolrn
Redevelopment Project Redevelopment Plan: 1) A fee interest in
real property located at 106 East 17th Street, improved with a
commercial structure and having a fair market value of over
$100,000. This property is rented to B&w Machine shop, producing
an annual income of over $10,000. 2) A fee interest in real
property located at 34 East 17th Street, improved with a comnercial
structure and having a fair market value of greater than $100,000.
3) A fee interest in real property located at 41 East 18th Street,
improved with a commercial structure and having a fair market value
of greater than $100r000. This property is rented to Southland
Clutch Company, producing an annual income of over $10r000. 4)
A fee interest in real property located at 25 East 17th Street,
improved with a commercial structure and having a fair market value
of greater than $100r000. This property is rented to Bush Porr,e r
Brake, producing an annual income of over $10r000. 5) A fee
interest in real property located at 115 East 17th Street, improved


with a commercial structure and having a fair rnarket value of over
$1001000. 6) A fee interest in real property located at 115 East
18th Street, improved with a commercial structure and having a fair
market value of over $100,000. This property is rented to Sikkens,
Inc., producing an annual income of over $10,000. 7) 
A fee
interest in real property located at 110 East 15th Street, improved
vrith a commercial structure and having a fair market value of over
$100r000. This property is rented to painters' Supply, producing
an annual income of over $10,000. 8) A fee interest in real
property locaEed at 125 East 17th Street, improved with a
commercial structure and having a fair market value of over
$100,000. This property is used as the place of business for
Vancon Corporation, a closely held corporation of vihich Vice-Mayor
Van Deventer is the majority stockholder.
In addition to the above real property interests, Vice-Mayor Van
Deventer owns the following real property interests, located within
the City limits but outside the boundaries of the Redevelopnent
Project Area3 1) a personal residence at 1706 Prospect Streeti
2) a commercial structure at 1540 I{arbj.son Avenue.
None of Vice-t'layor Van Deventer rs properties is located within 2500
feet of any of the acguisition areas designated in Amendment III
of the Redevelopment P1an.
Councilman Michael R. Da1la ot^rns a personal residence at 1020 "C"
Avenue, which is within the City of National City, and within the
boundaries of the Redevelopment Project Area. This property is
located within two blocks of the southeast boundary of Phase II of
Amendnent IfI of the Redevelopment P1an. However, this property
is not located within 2500 feet of any of the acquisition areas
designated in Amendment III.
Councilman Ered Pruitt owns a personal residence al 2414 East Igth
Street, which is within the City of National City, but outside the
boundaries of the Redevelopment Project Area. This property is not
located within 2500 feet of any of the acguisitj.on areas designated
in Amendment III.
Additionally, 
Councilman Pruitt 
has
a leasehold interest in
property located at 1714 East 8th Street. This property is within
the city limits and r^rithin the boundaries of the Redevelopment
Project Area, but is not located within 2500 feet of any of the
acquisition areas designated in Amendment III. 
This property is
improved wj.th a commercial building, which is used as the place of
business for a hardware store of which Counci.lman pruitt is the
propr iet or .
A map of National City is enclosed as Exhibit 
Said map
depicts the aforementioned real property interests, as well as
Acquisition Areas rAn Lhrough nG", and nphase If" of Amendment IfI
of the Redevelopment P1an.


ISSUE PRESE}.TTED
Are Mayor Waters, Vice-!,layor Van Deventer, Councilrnan Dalla, or
Councilman Pruitt precluded from participating in action(s) to
amend the 
National City 
Dorrntown Redevelopment project
Redevelopment PIan, by virtue of the the financial interests
discussed above, and in view of the provisions of Section 18702(c)
(the nPublic cenerally Exceptionr), and Section I8702.1(a) (3) (D)
and (c) (2) of the Regulations of the pair political 
practices
Commis sion?
More specifically, are the above officials subject to a "blanket
disqualificationn 
due to their 
financial 
interests 
in 
the
Redevelopment Project Area, or should di squal i ficat ion more
properly be considered on a ntransactionaln basis, i.e., 
based on
the nature of the amendment to the Redevelopment plan being
considered?
JUSTIFICATION FOR COMMISSION OP INION
The issuance of a commission opinion is warranted under Government
Code Section 83I14(a) and Sections 18320-18327 of the Regulations
due to the somewhat unique fact situation presented, namely, the
fact that 2.083 acres - approximately 44? of the City,s land area -
is located within the boundaries of the existing Redevelopment
Project Area.
Very truly yours,
A*a€;*
George H. Eiser, f II
City Attorn ey
cHElpj 1
a/

AA.,L

sections.question: Are Mayor Waters, Vice-Mayor Van Deventer, Councilman Dalla, or Councilman Pruitt precluded from participating in action(s) to amend the National City Downtown Redevelopment Project Redevelopment Plan, by virtue of the financial interests discussed above, and in view of the provisions of Section 18702(c) (the "Public Generally Exception"), and Section 18702.1(a) (3) (D) and (c) (2) of the Regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission? More specifically, are the above officials subject to a "blanket disqualification" due to their financial interests in the Redevelopment Project Area, or should disqualification more properly be considered on a "transactional" basis, i.e., based on the nature of the amendment to the Redevelopment Plan being considered?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether city councilmembers with real property interests in a redevelopment project area are subject to blanket disqualification or transactional-basis disqualification when voting on amendments to the redevelopment plan, considering the "Public Generally Exception" and other FPPC regulations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. Detailed factual background provided about councilmembers' real property holdings. No FPPC conclusion or advice provided in this document. Request submitted pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(a) and FPPC Regulations Sections 18320-18327.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Are Mayor Waters, Vice-Mayor Van Deventer, Councilman Dalla, or Councilman Pruitt precluded from participating in action(s) to amend the National City Downtown Redevelopment Project Redevelopment Plan, by virtue of the financial interests discussed above, and in view of the provisions of Section 18702(c) (the "Public Generally Exception"), and Section 18702.1(a) (3) (D) and (c) (2) of the Regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission? More specifically, are the above officials subject to a "blanket disqualification" due to their financial interests in the Redevelopment Project Area, or should disqualification more properly be considered on a "transactional" basis, i.e., based on the nature of the amendment to the Redevelopment Plan being considered?

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from the City of National City's City Attorney requesting an FPPC opinion on whether four city councilmembers with substantial real property interests within the National City Downtown Redevelopment Project Area are precluded from participating in votes on proposed Amendment III to the Redevelopment Plan.
